data.body:
EPIC DELIVERY RETROSPECTIVE — jira-reb-1160

Scope and setup
- Executed the decomposed epic Keep plan-review attestations current across relationships and execution using the repository rebar-implement protocol.
- Read and followed AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, the CLAUDE.md import guidance, gate documentation, and the Gerrit workflow.
- Worked from fresh isolated worktrees based on current origin/main, used each worktree virtualenv explicitly, claimed the epic and each story before work, and kept ticket events on the tickets branch separate from Gerrit code review.
- Tree delivered bottom-up: seven stories and two discovered bugs, all direct children of the epic.

Landed child work
- acaf-bb4d-9eba-470c — canonical signed related-material pins: 32131209a.
- 75de-02b0-d454-410b — optional pin enforcement and compatibility/completion proofs: 0c1d0c184, 41c57ae3f, 58ae8bd86.
- 5967-4476-7f9f-442d — planning/execution review phases: 94e605a29.
- 2bbd-87f6-8765-4b00 — configurable current-plan-review close gate: 7cec0f64c.
- 7c27-10fc-4fe6-4b56 — focused prerequisite evaluation and packing fidelity gate: f373cf4a6, 8b6df6a77.
- cb6b-24b4-1056-4ee6 — shared derived health across CLI, MCP, audit, web, and advanced docs: dcf41e782, Gerrit 913.
- 9417-a700-297d-4701 — accept runner metadata in prerequisite payloads: 60a0eab88.
- e833-965d-13de-43a2 — align hierarchy precheck fixture with required review phase: f2e4247a1.
- 145e-52a9-26e3-4209 — enable dogfooding policy and prove lifecycle behavior: 7650f579b, 365345897, 73efe1e68 through Gerrit 922, 923, and 924.

Held-out TDD and review evidence
- For cb6b, authored the visible and physically held-out surface oracle RED-first. A standard implementer required two feedback cycles and then a fresh strong implementer. Final surface oracle reached 26 passed with 2 optional UI skips. Mutations against legacy/no-target inference and signature verification made held-out tests fail, then exact restoration returned green. Independent review found and fixed authoritative signature verification, explicit nested schemas, and advisory-versus-disabled presentation.
- For 145e, inventoried existing coverage, authored three visible happy-path and three physically held-out real-CLI tests, and confirmed all six RED because both project policy flags were disabled. After implementation, the real-CLI lifecycle oracle passed. Mutations for stale health and planning-close behavior were killed. Later completion findings expanded the public/private oracle to 15 passing lifecycle tests.
- Additional mutation probes killed narrow transitive invalidation, inbound blocks normalization, return-to-open phase reset, close-profile code freshness, enable-disable-reenable behavior, archived/deleted distinction, unchanged re-review propagation, and execution-time self-edit freshness.
- Independent code reviews approved both final follow-ups. Gerrit 923 and 924 each received LLM-Review +1, Verified +1, and zero unresolved comments; all merged changes passed the project review gates before landing.

Gate remediation and operational issues
- The 7c27 completion verifier initially failed on eight missing proof areas: named contract tests, signal-chain tests, finder exhaustion/batching/index coverage, comparative baseline API/CLI behavior, baseline metadata, scenario gold corpus, and batch relation-snapshot seams. These were implemented and verified rather than bypassed.
- Gerrit 913 CI exposed an outdated detailed-show compatibility probe; the oracle was updated, the isolated probe passed 140 of 140, and patchset 2 landed green.
- The first 145e completion verification correctly failed on missing public lifecycle evidence for transitive chains, depends_on plus inbound-blocks dedupe, return-to-open reset, code-only close freshness, enable-disable-reenable, and deleted versus archived targets. Two reviewed follow-ups supplied those proofs. No ticket was force-closed.
- The first epic close correctly rejected stale plan material. The epic plan was updated to describe the shipped full fresh reducer snapshot under lock, current-no-relationships health, and the specialized 0.60 prerequisite-consistency threshold. A fresh execution-phase plan review passed and was signed at 73efe1e68; refreshed completion verification then passed all 22 parent criteria.
- Several transient provider transport errors were retried. One GitHub SSH reset was misreported as missing credentials; a direct git fetch origin immediately succeeded and confirmed credentials were valid. Background terminals were restarted when they stopped.
- Codex-specific prevention guidance was added and tested in AGENTS.md: explicit worktree virtualenv use in 0499d93fe, fallback behavior for unauthorized feature branches in e13692f59, and Gerrit REST credential reuse through the configured Git helper plus correct 401 diagnosis in 0e2c981f8.

Final verification and closure
- Focused public/private lifecycle oracle: 15 passed.
- First full suite: 7083 passed, 29 skipped, 112 deselected.
- Final full suite: 7084 passed, 29 skipped, 112 deselected.
- make check passed Ruff, format checks, DCO, workflow validation, and mypy over 369 source files.
- Final origin/main commit: 73efe1e68e20f35079f1627655ec946328c8c6cc.
- Story 145e-52a9-26e3-4209 passed completion verification and closed.
- Epic jira-reb-1160 passed refreshed plan review and completion verification and closed.
- Final audit confirmed all nine direct descendants closed and current epic health with enforced pins and a compatible execution phase.
- Related session logs: d01d-6bbf-69df-419d, b352-b1c2-b739-4935, and this consolidated log 082f-aef1-ed20-4f05.
